title: carex × pseudohelvola
intro: Carex × pseudohelvola, also known as False Helvella Sedge, is a hybrid plant species within the sedge family that is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carex × pseudohelvola include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its antiseptic properties.
